crash_point_name: Mid-LLM inference
openclaw_persisted_before_crash: User message that triggered this turn
why_openclaw_persistence_is_insufficient: Streaming response buffer is in-process memory only
Partial assistant message is never written to transcript until turn completes
No checkpoint of inference progress exists
tenure_checkpoint_required: None — inference is owned by OpenClaw, not Tenure
Tenure only activates when the LLM emits a tool_use decision
replay_safe_reason: No side effects during inference. Restart re-infers from scratch. The LLM may produce a different response, but no external state was mutated.

test_case.description: SIGKILL during LLM streaming, verify restart produces a valid new response
test_case.setup: Start agent turn with a prompt that triggers LLM inference; SIGKILL during streaming
test_case.assertion: On restart, agent starts a new turn from the last persisted transcript state; no side effects from the interrupted inference

crash_point_name: Tool decision emitted, execution not started
openclaw_persisted_before_crash: Prior transcript entries including the assistant message with tool_use block
why_openclaw_persistence_is_insufficient: The tool_use decision may be in the streaming buffer but not yet flushed to .jsonl
Even if flushed, the transcript records intent, not execution state
On restart, the LLM may re-infer and choose a different tool or different parameters
tenure_checkpoint_required: Tool call ID (deterministic, from workflow state)
Tool name
Serialized parameters
Execution type classification from SER router
replay_safe_reason: Tool call IDs are generated with crypto.randomUUID() (src/agents/tools/agent-step.ts) or randomBytes (src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts:1772-1778). On replay, different IDs would be generated, breaking idempotency dedup.

test_case.description: SIGKILL after tool_use decision but before dispatch; verify deterministic replay selects same tool
test_case.setup: Workflow receives tool_use from LLM, schedules Activity; SIGKILL before Activity starts
test_case.assertion: On replay, workflow re-schedules the same Activity with same parameters; no side effects from the gap

crash_point_name: Mid-tool execution — idempotent read (web search, file read, grep)
openclaw_component: Agent Runtime -> Tool Execution -> Read Path
why_openclaw_persistence_is_insufficient: In-flight read result exists only in process memory
web-fetch FETCH_CACHE is an in-memory Map (src/agents/tools/web-fetch.ts)
No checkpoint of partial or complete read results
state_in_memory_only: In-memory cache entries with Date.now()-based TTL
tenure_checkpoint_required: Tool call ID
Tool name and parameters
Serialized read result (Activity completion in Temporal history)
replay_safe_reason: Reads are idempotent. Re-execution is safe but wasteful. Temporal Activity caching eliminates the re-execution entirely — replay returns cached result from Event History.

test_case.description: Web search Activity completes, Worker SIGKILL before result delivery, replay returns cached result
test_case.setup: Execute web_search Activity; SIGKILL Worker after Activity completion recorded in Temporal
test_case.assertion: No second HTTP request; cached result returned on replay; workflow continues

crash_point_name: Mid-tool execution — file write (fs.writeFile, file create, file edit)
openclaw_component: Agent Runtime -> Tool Execution -> Node Host -> File System
openclaw_persisted_before_crash: The file itself may be partially or fully written to disk
why_openclaw_persistence_is_insufficient: File write may have completed but tool result is not yet in .jsonl
No record of whether the write succeeded, failed, or partially completed
sendInvokeResult errors are ignored (best-effort delivery to gateway)
On restart, no mechanism to detect the write already happened
tenure_checkpoint_required: Tool call ID
File path + content hash (idempotency key)
Serialized write result
Pre-write file state for compensation (revert)
replay_safe_reason: With Temporal: Activity result in Event History prevents re-execution. Idempotency key (file_path + content_hash) guards against duplicate writes even if Activity is re-dispatched.

test_case.description: File write Activity completes, Worker SIGKILL, replay returns cached result without rewriting
test_case.setup: Execute file-write Activity writing 'hello' to test.txt; SIGKILL after Activity completion
test_case.assertion: test.txt contains 'hello' exactly once; file hash unchanged; workflow continues from cached result

crash_point_name: Mid-tool execution — external API call (Stripe, GitHub, Slack)
why_openclaw_persistence_is_insufficient: HTTP request may have reached the external service and produced a side effect
No pre-execution checkpoint of request parameters
No idempotency key persisted that survives process restart
Gateway dedupe Map is in-memory only (src/gateway/server-runtime-state.ts)
pdf-native-providers.ts uses direct fetch() with no dedup guard
tenure_checkpoint_required: Tool call ID
API endpoint and parameters
Idempotency key (derived from call semantics, not UUID)
Serialized API response
Compensation action (e.g., refund, delete, close)
temporal_primitive: Activity (idempotency key) or Saga (for critical transactions)
replay_safe_reason: With Temporal: Activity result prevents re-execution. For critical APIs (Stripe), a Saga pattern with compensation ensures exactly-once semantics even when the external service lacks native idempotency.

test_case.description: API call Activity completes, Worker dies, replay returns cached response without re-calling API
test_case.setup: Execute API-call Activity with idempotency key; SIGKILL after completion
test_case.assertion: No second API call; cached response returned; external service state unchanged

crash_point_name: Mid-tool execution — browser/Playwright session
why_openclaw_persistence_is_insufficient: Browser tab registry (trackedTabsBySession) is an in-memory Map — not persisted
DOM state, scroll position, form inputs, cookies are in the browser process, not on disk
Cleanup (cleanupBrowserSessionsForLifecycleEnd) runs best-effort via runBestEffortCleanup — skipped on SIGKILL
Browser/Chromium process may be orphaned with no mechanism to reconnect
state_in_memory_only: Page DOM, scroll position, form state
tenure_checkpoint_required: Session ID for the browser context
Current URL and navigation state
Heartbeat payload with interaction progress
Activity result for each discrete browser action
replay_safe_reason: Browser DOM state cannot be reconstructed from Temporal history alone. Session must restart from URL. Heartbeat payload should include enough context to resume from a meaningful checkpoint, not from scratch.

test_case.description: Browser session child workflow with heartbeat; Worker SIGKILL; new Worker resumes from last heartbeat checkpoint
test_case.setup: Start browser child workflow navigating through 3 pages; SIGKILL after page 2 heartbeat
test_case.assertion: New Worker resumes from page 2 URL; browser re-launched; remaining actions complete; no duplicate page interactions

crash_point_name: Mid-tool execution — sandbox/container execution
openclaw_persisted_before_crash: Sandbox registry JSON on disk (containerName, sessionKey, configHash)
why_openclaw_persistence_is_insufficient: Docker container keeps running after host SIGKILL — but host loses orchestration state
In-flight exec command output is lost
Registry tracks container existence but not execution progress within a container
No checkpoint of which sandbox command was running or its partial output
state_in_memory_only: Orchestration state linking sandbox result to agent turn
tenure_checkpoint_required: Sandbox session ID
Command being executed
Activity result for the sandbox execution
Container name for reconnect on resume
temporal_primitive: Activity (isolated, with container reconnect on retry)
replay_safe_reason: With Temporal: Activity wraps the sandbox exec. If Worker dies, replay returns cached result. If Activity was mid-execution, retry reconnects to the running container using the persisted registry.

test_case.description: Sandbox exec Activity running; Worker SIGKILL; container survives; new Worker reconnects
test_case.setup: Start sandbox Activity executing a script; SIGKILL host after container starts but before exec completes
test_case.assertion: Container still running; retry Activity reconnects and collects output; no duplicate container creation

crash_point_name: Tool complete, result not delivered to next reasoning step
openclaw_persisted_before_crash: Side effect already completed (file written, API called, etc.)
why_openclaw_persistence_is_insufficient: Completed tool result is in SessionManager pending buffer, not yet in .jsonl
flushPendingToolResultsAfterIdle has 30s timeout; may clear instead of flush (clearPendingOnTimeout: true)
sendInvokeResult / sendNodeEvent errors are ignored (best-effort)
On restart, the tool result is gone. The side effect happened. No record exists to prevent re-execution.
state_in_memory_only: Completed tool result in SessionManager pending buffer
Pending delivery state to next reasoning step
Tool call ID that links result to the dispatched call
tenure_checkpoint_required: Tool call ID
Serialized completed result (already in Temporal Event History as Activity completion)
This is the canonical crash point that Temporal replay solves directly
temporal_primitive: Activity (result already recorded in Event History)
replay_safe_reason: This is Temporal's core value proposition. The Activity completed and its result is in Event History. On replay, the workflow receives the cached result without re-executing the tool. Zero re-execution, zero duplicate side effects.

test_case.description: The canonical no-duplicate proof: file write completes, Worker dies before result delivery, replay resumes
test_case.setup: Execute file-write Activity; SIGKILL Worker after Activity completion but before workflow advances
test_case.assertion: File written exactly once; cached result returned on replay; workflow continues to next step

crash_point_name: Tool complete, result not delivered — critical transaction (Stripe charge, Terraform apply)
openclaw_component: Agent Runtime -> Result Delivery -> Critical Path
openclaw_persisted_before_crash: External side effect completed and is IRREVERSIBLE (charge created, infra deployed)
why_openclaw_persistence_is_insufficient: Same gap as CP-008 but consequences are irreversible
A Stripe charge cannot be un-charged by simply retrying
A Terraform apply may have created real infrastructure
Without a durable record of completion, re-execution could charge twice or deploy twice
state_in_memory_only: Charge ID / resource ID that would be needed for compensation
HITL approval state that authorized the action
tenure_checkpoint_required: Tool call ID
Idempotency key (charge_id, resource_id)
Serialized result with external resource identifiers
Compensation action registration (RefundCharge, terraform destroy)
HITL approval record
replay_safe_reason: Saga pattern: Activity records completion in Temporal history. Compensation Activity is registered. On replay, completed Activity returns cached result. If downstream steps fail, compensation runs the registered rollback (refund, destroy).

test_case.description: Critical transaction completes, Worker dies, replay resumes without re-executing the transaction
test_case.setup: Execute Stripe-charge Saga Activity with HITL approval; SIGKILL after charge succeeds
test_case.assertion: No second charge; charge_id in cached result; compensation registered; workflow continues

crash_point_name: Result delivered to reasoning step, not flushed to .jsonl
openclaw_persisted_before_crash: Prior transcript entries (missing the current tool result)
why_openclaw_persistence_is_insufficient: Tool result was used for the current LLM turn but not persisted to .jsonl
replay-history.ts createProviderReplaySessionState catches and IGNORES persistence failures
On restart, the transcript is missing this result, creating a gap in conversation history
If the LLM used this result to make a decision that led to a side effect, the decision chain is broken
state_in_memory_only: Tool result in SessionManager pending buffer
LLM reasoning state that consumed this result
tenure_checkpoint_required: Already handled: if the Activity result is in Temporal Event History, this crash point is irrelevant
Temporal replay reconstructs the full execution chain regardless of .jsonl state
replay_safe_reason: Temporal history is the authority, not .jsonl. Even if the transcript flush fails, Temporal replay reconstructs the correct execution state from Activity completions in Event History.

test_case.description: Tool result delivered but transcript flush fails; Temporal replay reconstructs state correctly
test_case.setup: Execute Activity, deliver result to workflow, simulate .jsonl flush failure, SIGKILL
test_case.assertion: Temporal replay returns all Activity results from history; workflow continues correctly regardless of .jsonl state

crash_point_name: Compaction mid-rewrite
openclaw_component: Agent Runtime -> Compaction -> Session Transcript Rewrite
openclaw_persisted_before_crash: Possible temp file with new compacted content
why_openclaw_persistence_is_insufficient: Compaction uses temp file + fs.rename pattern — atomic on most filesystems but not guaranteed
If crash between copyFile to archive and rename of temp, the .jsonl may be in an intermediate state
Compaction depends on model summarization output which is non-deterministic (blocker B-2 from Session 1)
On replay, compaction would produce a different summary, causing workflow divergence
tenure_checkpoint_required: Compaction must be a side-effecting Activity
The summary output must be recorded in Temporal history
On replay, the cached summary is returned instead of re-summarizing
replay_safe_reason: By treating compaction as an Activity, the non-deterministic summary output is recorded once and replayed deterministically. The .jsonl rewrite becomes a secondary effect.

test_case.description: Compaction Activity completes, Worker dies mid-rewrite, replay returns cached summary
test_case.setup: Trigger compaction Activity; SIGKILL during .jsonl rewrite
test_case.assertion: Replay returns same summary from Temporal history; .jsonl is repaired on next startup

crash_point_name: SKILL.md loading interrupted
openclaw_component: Agent Runtime -> Skill Loading -> Workspace Resolution
openclaw_persisted_before_crash: SKILL.md files on disk (unchanged by loading)
why_openclaw_persistence_is_insufficient: Skill snapshot (SkillSnapshot) is a derived in-memory artifact, not persisted
workspaceVersions and globalVersion in refresh-state.ts are in-memory counters
Half-built snapshot is lost; no durable loading transaction
On restart, skills are re-read from disk (safe but adds startup latency)
tenure_checkpoint_required: Skill resolution should be an Activity that records the resolved skill set
Router classification depends on skill metadata; caching the resolution prevents non-determinism
replay_safe_reason: SKILL.md files on disk are the source of truth and are unchanged by loading. Re-reading on restart is safe. As an Activity, the resolved skill set is cached in Temporal history for deterministic replay.

test_case.description: Skill resolution Activity caches resolved skill set; Worker restart does not re-resolve
test_case.setup: Execute skill-resolution Activity; SIGKILL during loading; restart Worker
test_case.assertion: If Activity completed: cached result returned. If not: Activity retries and re-reads from disk.

crash_point_name: Cron timer fires, job execution not started
openclaw_persisted_before_crash: nextRunAtMs and lastRunAtMs from prior runs
why_openclaw_persistence_is_insufficient: setTimeout timer handle is in-memory — lost on SIGKILL
runningAtMs marker indicates dispatch intent, not execution start
On restart, ops.start clears stale runningAtMs and calls runMissedJobs
Catch-up re-executes from scratch — no way to know if the original dispatch had any effect
tenure_checkpoint_required: Temporal Schedule owns trigger timing
Schedule survives Worker death independently
No in-process timer needed
replay_safe_reason: Temporal Schedule fires independently of the Worker process. If the Worker is down, the Schedule queues the trigger. When the Worker restarts, catch-up executes according to the Schedule's overlap policy.

test_case.description: Temporal Schedule fires while Worker is down; Worker restarts and catches up
test_case.setup: Configure Schedule for every 60s; SIGKILL Worker; wait 2 minutes; restart Worker
test_case.assertion: Schedule fired during downtime; Worker processes missed triggers per policy; no gaps in execution

crash_point_name: Cron job mid-execution — recurring job
openclaw_component: Cron Service -> Timer -> Job Execution -> Agent Turn
openclaw_persisted_before_crash: Partial agent turn transcript if any tool results were flushed
why_openclaw_persistence_is_insufficient: Job execution state is in gateway process async flow with no durability wrapping
Side effects from the interrupted agent turn may have completed
On restart, catch-up via runMissedJobs re-executes the job from scratch
allowCronMissedRunByLastRun creates a new run without checking prior side effects
No dedup between the interrupted run and the catch-up run
state_in_memory_only: Tool results from the interrupted turn
Job outcome (not yet applied via applyJobResult)
tenure_checkpoint_required: Each cron-triggered agent turn is a Temporal Workflow execution
Tool calls within the turn are Activities with idempotency keys
If Worker dies mid-turn, replay resumes from the last completed Activity
No catch-up re-execution needed — the original workflow resumes
replay_safe_reason: Each scheduled trigger starts a Workflow. Activities within the Workflow are checkpointed. Worker death causes replay from the last Activity completion. No duplicate side effects.

test_case.description: The canonical cron durability proof from RESEARCH-SETUP.md
test_case.setup: Configure 60s schedule appending timestamped lines to log.txt; run 3 cycles; SIGKILL; miss 2 cycles; restart
test_case.assertion: log.txt has expected line count; no gaps from Worker death; no duplicates from catch-up; sequence numbers correct

crash_point_name: Cron one-shot job interrupted
why_openclaw_persistence_is_insufficient: One-shot interrupted jobs are explicitly NOT retried on restart
ops.ts:107-134 collects interruptedOneShotIds and passes them as skipJobIds to runMissedJobs
The job's side effects may have partially completed but will never be retried or compensated
This is a documented design choice in OpenClaw, not a bug — but it means one-shot reliability is not guaranteed
state_in_memory_only: Partial side effects from the interrupted run
tenure_checkpoint_required: One-shot scheduled action becomes a Temporal Workflow
If interrupted, replay resumes from last Activity completion
No explicit skip — the Workflow simply continues
replay_safe_reason: Temporal Workflows resume after Worker restart. A one-shot Workflow that was interrupted continues from its last checkpoint, unlike OpenClaw which abandons it.

test_case.description: One-shot scheduled Workflow interrupted mid-execution; Worker restart resumes it
test_case.setup: Schedule a one-shot Workflow with 3 Activity steps; SIGKILL after step 2
test_case.assertion: Worker restart replays steps 1-2 from history; executes step 3; Workflow completes

crash_point_name: Gateway restart — mid-agent-run
why_openclaw_persistence_is_insufficient: All WS client connections dropped — clients Set is process-local
Broadcast seq counter resets — clients cannot detect gap size
Dedupe Map lost — same idempotency key can be accepted again after restart
agentRunSeq Map lost — run ordering state gone
Events are not replayed — official docs confirm clients must refresh on gaps
Any in-flight agent run that depended on Gateway coordination is broken
tenure_checkpoint_required: Tenure Workflows do not depend on Gateway for execution coordination
Temporal Server is the coordination authority, not the Gateway process
Gateway becomes a UI/notification layer, not the execution layer
temporal_primitive: N/A — Gateway is outside the Tenure execution boundary
replay_safe_reason: By moving execution coordination to Temporal, Gateway restart only affects UI/notification delivery, not execution correctness. Workflows continue regardless of Gateway state.

test_case.description: Gateway restart during Tenure Workflow execution; Workflow continues unaffected
test_case.setup: Start a multi-step Workflow; SIGKILL Gateway mid-execution; restart Gateway
test_case.assertion: Workflow completes all steps; Gateway reconnect shows updated state; no execution interruption

crash_point_name: Subagent spawn — parent dies before child completes
openclaw_component: Agent Runtime -> Subagent Registry -> Session Spawn
openclaw_persisted_before_crash: Subagent runs.json on disk (containerName, sessionKey, requesterSessionKey)
Child transcript .jsonl (if any tool results were flushed)
why_openclaw_persistence_is_insufficient: In-memory subagentRuns Map may have entries not yet persisted to runs.json
Pending timers (pendingLifecycleErrorByRunId), sweeper state, resumedRuns — lost
On restart, restoreSubagentRunsOnce reloads from disk but in-flight state is gone
Orphan recovery (scheduleSubagentOrphanRecovery) is best-effort and SIGUSR1-related
Child may complete while parent is dead — result has no parent to receive it
state_in_memory_only: subagentRuns Map (partially — disk snapshot may lag)
tenure_checkpoint_required: Parent-child relationship modeled as parent Workflow -> child Workflow
ParentClosePolicy determines child behavior on parent death
Child completion signals parent Workflow via Temporal, not in-process callbacks
replay_safe_reason: Temporal's parent-child Workflow relationship survives Worker death. Child completion is recorded in Temporal history. Parent replay receives the child result from history.

test_case.description: Parent Workflow spawns child; parent Worker dies; child completes; parent resumes and receives result
test_case.setup: Start parent Workflow that spawns child Workflow; SIGKILL parent Worker; child completes independently
test_case.assertion: Parent Worker restarts; replay reaches child-spawn point; receives child result from history; continues

crash_point_name: Multi-agent message delivery — sender dies after send
openclaw_component: Agent Runtime -> sessions_send -> Gateway agent RPC
openclaw_persisted_before_crash: Target session may have received the message (if Gateway processed it)
Sender transcript may or may not include the send result
why_openclaw_persistence_is_insufficient: sessions_send uses crypto.randomUUID() for idempotency key per invocation
Gateway dedupe Map is in-memory only — restart allows re-acceptance of same logical send
A2A flow uses separate randomUUID() per announce send — no long-lived idempotency
delivery field in tool result is ephemeral { status: 'pending', mode: 'announce' } — not a durable receipt
If sender dies after send reaches Gateway but before confirmation, no record of successful delivery
state_in_memory_only: Gateway dedupe Map entry for this send
In-flight agent run on the target session
tenure_checkpoint_required: Inter-agent send becomes a Signal to the target Workflow
Signal delivery is recorded in Temporal history on both sides
Sender Activity completes when Signal is accepted by Temporal Server
replay_safe_reason: Temporal Signals are durable. The send Activity records Signal dispatch in history. The receiving Workflow records Signal receipt. Both survive Worker death.

test_case.description: Agent A sends Signal to Agent B; A's Worker dies after send; B receives and processes; A resumes
test_case.setup: Workflow A sends Signal to Workflow B; SIGKILL A's Worker after Signal dispatch
test_case.assertion: Signal delivered to B; A's replay returns send confirmation from history; no duplicate send

crash_point_name: MCP bridge — mid-tool-call via external MCP server
openclaw_component: MCP -> Channel Bridge -> External MCP Server
why_openclaw_persistence_is_insufficient: MCP bridge queue (max 1000 events), pendingWaiters, pendingApprovals — all in-memory
Tool call ID is mcp-${Date.now()} — not stable, not persisted, not idempotent
close().catch(() => undefined) swallows shutdown errors
sendNotification errors suppressed except optional stderr
Entire MCP state is lost on SIGKILL with no recovery path
tenure_checkpoint_required: MCP tool call wrapped in Activity
Tool call ID derived from workflow state (not Date.now())
Activity result persisted in Temporal history
replay_safe_reason: MCP tool call becomes a Temporal Activity. The MCP-specific state is ephemeral and reconstructed per-call. Activity result in Temporal history is the durable record.

test_case.description: MCP tool Activity completes; Worker SIGKILL; replay returns cached result
test_case.setup: Execute MCP tool call as Activity; SIGKILL after completion
test_case.assertion: No second MCP call; cached result from Temporal history; no orphaned MCP state

crash_point_name: Gateway dedupe map lost — duplicate tool calls accepted after restart
why_openclaw_persistence_is_insufficient: Gateway dedupe Map is created at startup: dedupe = new Map()
Keys are like agent:${idempotencyKey} with in-memory lifetime only
Test 'agent dedupe survives reconnect' tests WS reconnect to same process, NOT process restart
After SIGKILL + restart, the same idempotencyKey is accepted again
This is the mechanism that should prevent duplicate tool calls but it does not survive crashes
tenure_checkpoint_required: Temporal's deterministic replay IS the dedup mechanism
Activity IDs are deterministic from workflow state
Completed Activities are never re-executed — Temporal history is the dedup guard
temporal_primitive: Temporal Event History (inherent dedup via replay)
replay_safe_reason: Temporal replay is inherently dedup-safe. Completed Activities return cached results. No external dedup map needed. The dedup is structural, not a fragile in-memory cache.

test_case.description: Verify Temporal replay prevents duplicate execution after Worker restart
test_case.setup: Execute 5 Activities in a Workflow; SIGKILL after Activity 3; restart Worker
test_case.assertion: Activities 1-3 return cached results; Activity 4-5 execute once each; total executions = 5, not 8

boundary_breaking_reason: This is not just a crash point — it is a fundamental architectural gap. OpenClaw's dedup mechanism is ephemeral. Tenure's dedup mechanism is structural via Temporal replay.

crash_point_name: Task registry — unsaved in-memory Maps lost
openclaw_persisted_before_crash: Task registry snapshot in SQLite (if upsert completed)
why_openclaw_persistence_is_insufficient: Primary working set is in-memory Maps; SQLite is populated via incremental upsert
If SIGKILL arrives between Map update and SQLite upsert, the latest state is lost
restoreTaskRegistryOnce failure: log.warn only, does not rethrow
Task flow observer errors: empty catch with comment 'must not break registry writes'
On restart, SQLite snapshot may be stale relative to actual task state
tenure_checkpoint_required: Task state managed as Workflow state in Temporal
Each task state transition is a Workflow signal or Activity
No separate task registry needed — Workflow state IS the registry
temporal_primitive: Workflow state (Queries for read, Signals for updates)
replay_safe_reason: Task state as Workflow state is inherently durable. Temporal replays reconstruct exact task state from Activity completions and Signal receipts. No SQLite sync gap.

test_case.description: Task state transitions survive Worker death via Workflow replay
test_case.setup: Create task Workflow with 5 state transitions; SIGKILL after transition 3
test_case.assertion: Replay reconstructs transitions 1-3 from history; transitions 4-5 execute normally
